Looking at the data provided, it's a list of test cases with their mutant IDs and status changes. My goal is to check for each mutant if its failure is adjacent, meaning it's part of a group where failures are connected.

Since I don't have access to the actual source code or the context of how these mutants are structured, I can only infer based on the mutant IDs. If multiple test cases share similar parts of the ID (like the same package or class), they might be adjacent.

For example, if several tests under "org.joda.time.DateTimeZone" fail, those could be adjacent because they're part of the same component. Conversely, if a test from a different package fails, it's less likely to be adjacent.

In this case, looking at the provided data, there are mutants from various packages like DateTimeZone, LocalDate, LocalDateTime, etc. Each seems isolated, so unless specified otherwise, I can assume they're non-adjacent.

Therefore, for each mutant in the list, "is_fault_adjacent" would be False because their failures don't seem to cluster around a single area.
